| publish.serialized.non_bundled | supported | partial | minor | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | byte-seam-only | na | na | pass | pass | rcl_bridge.c:122-130 crcl_publisher_create rejects any type absent from crcl_marshal_registry.c, so route (a) (typed/serialized via rmw) cannot open a publisher; on that miss RclClient.createRawWriterPublisher falls back to route (b) — the CDDSBridge raw-CDR writer below rmw (sibling participant on the context domain, DDS topic/type via SwiftROS2Wire, USER_DATA typehash). Served outside rcl's entity graph (a documented divergence) but interoperable with any ROS 2 subscriber by topic + DDS-type name. Proven in-process by crcl-nonbundled-loopback (route-b .rcl publish -> .dds wire subscribe, same domain) and by RclNonbundledPublishIntegrationTests (LAN). Unblocks Conduit AudioData + CompressedPointCloud2 zenoh-rmw variant caveat: route-b is CycloneDDS-only and unreachable via rmw_zenoh, so the zenoh variant throws unsupportedFeature on a registry miss instead of silently publishing onto an invisible DDS domain (#155 fail-loud gate); bundling (#154/#158) is the mitigation for every shipped type. |
| subscribe.serialized.sensor_msgs/Imu | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | pass | na | pass | na | rcl_subscription.c rcl_take_serialized_message -> (Data,UInt64) seam; Imu bundled in registry; Node.swift:110 CDRDecoder decode is identical across backends |
| subscribe.serialized.sensor_msgs/Image | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | na | na | pass | na | sensor_msgs/Image bundled in crcl_marshal_registry (#154) — serialized take is registry-gated and now resolves; registry-resolve guard in MarshalRegistryResolveTests |
| subscribe.serialized.sensor_msgs/PointCloud2 | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | pass | na | pass | na | PointCloud2 bundled in registry; rcl_subscription.c serialized take + Node.swift CDRDecoder |
| subscribe.serialized.non_bundled | supported | partial | minor | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | byte-seam-only | na | na | pass | na | RclClient.createSubscription falls back to a CDDSBridge raw-CDR reader on a registry miss (mirror of the publish route-b): a sibling participant on the context domain, DDS topic/type via SwiftROS2Wire + USER_DATA typehash; received below rmw (outside rcl's wait-set, a documented divergence) but interoperable with any ROS 2 publisher by topic + DDS-type name. Proven in-process by crcl-nonbundled-sub-loopback (.dds wire publish -> .rcl route-b subscribe, same domain) and by RclNonbundledSubscribeIntegrationTests (LAN). Pairs with publish.serialized.non_bundled for full non-bundled pub/sub. zenoh-rmw variant caveat: route-b is CycloneDDS-only and unreachable via rmw_zenoh, so the zenoh variant throws unsupportedFeature on a registry miss instead of silently publishing onto an invisible DDS domain (#155 fail-loud gate); bundling (#154/#158) is the mitigation for every shipped type. |
| service.server | supported | partial | minor | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | na | na | pass | na | rcl_service.c crcl_service_create gated on crcl_srv_registry_lookup (12 bundled srv types, crcl_srv_registry.c); no byte-seam fallback for services. Wire path serves any ROS2ServiceType Non-bundled (arbitrary-type) services deferred-by-decision — no consumer (Conduit is a publisher); the pure-Swift wire path serves any ROS2ServiceType; a route-b raw-CDR fallback would require request/response GUID correlation for no current need (design 2026-06-13 §9 non-goal). Bundled types fully supported. |
| service.client | supported | partial | minor | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | na | na | pass | na | rcl_client.c:135-143 crcl_client_create gated on the 12-entry srv registry; ROS2Client is generic over any service on the wire path Non-bundled (arbitrary-type) services deferred-by-decision — no consumer (Conduit is a publisher); the pure-Swift wire path serves any ROS2ServiceType; a route-b raw-CDR fallback would require request/response GUID correlation for no current need (design 2026-06-13 §9 non-goal). Bundled types fully supported. |
| param.declare | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | n-a | na | na | pass | na | backend-agnostic ParameterStore actor; declareParameter identical across backends |
| param.getset | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | n-a | na | na | pass | na | ParameterStore get/set is backend-agnostic; served via bundled rcl_interfaces param services (crcl_srv_registry.c) on RCL |
| param.event | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | n-a | na | na | pass | na | /parameter_events via installParameterEventEmitter + lazy ParameterEvent publisher (rcl_interfaces/msg/ParameterEvent bundled in crcl_marshal_registry.c) |
| action.server | supported | partial | minor | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | na | na | pass | na | rcl_action_server.c:212-220 crcl_action_server_create gated on crcl_action_registry_lookup; registry holds 1 entry (example_interfaces/action/Fibonacci). Wire path serves any action Non-bundled (arbitrary-type) actions deferred-by-decision — no consumer (Conduit is a publisher); the pure-Swift wire path serves any action; a route-b fallback would require the full goal state machine (3 services + 2 topics) for no current need (design 2026-06-13 §9 non-goal). Bundled types (Fibonacci) fully supported. |
| action.client | supported | partial | minor | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | na | na | pass | na | rcl_action_client.c crcl_action_client_create gated on crcl_action_registry (CRCL_ACTION_REGISTRY_ENTRY_COUNT 1, Fibonacci only) Non-bundled (arbitrary-type) actions deferred-by-decision — no consumer (Conduit is a publisher); the pure-Swift wire path serves any action; a route-b fallback would require the full goal state machine (3 services + 2 topics) for no current need (design 2026-06-13 §9 non-goal). Bundled types (Fibonacci) fully supported. |
| qos.profiles | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | n-a | na | na | pass | na | makeCrclQoS maps reliability/durability/history/depth -> rmw_qos_profile; same knob set as pure-Swift TransportQoS (deadline/lifespan/liveliness absent on both = parity) |
| transport.dds | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS/Linux | n-a | na | na | pass | na | RclTransportSession.open forwards ddsUnicastPeers (bare host) + ddsNetworkInterface; RclClient.makeDiscoveryURIXML builds the SAME CycloneDDS discovery XML as the wire DDS path via the shared dds_bridge_build_domain_config_xml, exported as CYCLONEDDS_URI around context creation (restored on teardown). .rclUnicast factory + validate() peer check close the config hole. RclDiscoveryEnvTests asserts the XML shape; RclUnicastIntegrationTests is the LAN proof |
| transport.zenoh | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS/Linux | n-a | pass | pass | pass | pass | MZ1: .zenoh(locator:) now routes to RclTransportSession in the zenoh-rmw variant via makeDefaultSession #elseif SWIFT_ROS2_RCL_RMW_ZENOH (zenoh-pico carved out). The router locator is injected into rmw_zenoh_cpp through RclClient.makeZenohSessionConfigJSON5 + applyZenohSessionEnv (ZENOH_SESSION_CONFIG_URI + ZENOH_ROUTER_CHECK_ATTEMPTS, a save/restore mirror of the DDS CYCLONEDDS_URI path), threaded through the createContext seam and RclTransportSession.open. RclZenohSessionEnvTests covers config shape, env apply/restore, and open() locator forwarding; the api-stability gate is green (createContext is a package requirement, public surface unchanged). MZ3 verified: end-to-end vs a live rmw_zenohd router (Docker, jazzy 0.2.9) — latency/soak/correctness recorded on this row; resource recorded in the MZ4 iPhone batch (PASS, documented binary-size divergence — see docs/verification-runbook.md). Build-time variant origin: M9 #128. visionOS caveat: the zenoh variant ships no xros/xrsimulator slices (zenoh-c dep pnet_sys lacks visionOS support) — visionOS stays on the wire path or .dds. |
| publish.typed.sensor_msgs/CameraInfo | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | na | na | pass | na | crcl_marshal_registry typed marshal for sensor_msgs/CameraInfo (#154); byte-parity vs pure-Swift CDR (CrossBackendBytesTests) |
| publish.serialized.tf2_msgs/TFMessage | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | na | na | pass | na | tf2_msgs bundled into both xcframeworks (#158, PKGS_UP_TO + registry-only entry) — Conduit /tf_static publishes route-a over the rmw serialized seam with transient-local durability; MarshalRegistryResolveTests |
| publish.serialized.audio_common_msgs/AudioData | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | na | na | pass | na | audio_common_msgs vendored (ros-drivers/audio_common@db2770b, msg-only) + bundled (#158); registry-only entry publishes route-a over the rmw serialized seam; MarshalRegistryResolveTests |
| publish.serialized.point_cloud_interfaces/CompressedPointCloud2 | supported | supported | n-a-by-design | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | bundled | na | na | pass | na | point_cloud_interfaces vendored (ros-perception/point_cloud_transport_plugins@1e4490c = 4.0.4, msg-only) + bundled (#158) — Conduit's Draco type is now a plain upstream type on the RCL path; MarshalRegistryResolveTests |
| distro.support | supported | partial | minor | iOS/Catalyst/macOS/visionOS | n-a | na | na | na | na | pure-Swift wire codecs speak Humble/Jazzy/Kilted/Rolling (ROS2Distro); the RCL backend bundles the Jazzy stack only (CRos2Jazzy*). A non-Jazzy distro on an RCL-backed session is a documented limitation. |
| platform.linux | supported | partial | minor | Linux | n-a | na | na | na | na | pure-Swift wire ships + is CI-tested on Ubuntu 22.04/24.04 x86_64+aarch64. MZ5 landed the Linux RCL backend via a system ROS 2 install (ament prefix, not an xcframework): .zenoh -> rcl+rmw_zenoh_cpp and .dds/.rcl -> rcl+rmw_cyclonedds_cpp, with the rmw selected per-process at runtime via RMW_IMPLEMENTATION. Verified on Ubuntu 24.04 / ROS 2 Jazzy: crcl-loopback 20/20 in-process over cyclonedds; talker->listener 10/10 over a live rmw_zenohd router; SwiftROS2RCLTests green in the ci-rcl Linux leg. One process serves .zenoh OR .dds (the rmw is process-global) — switching transports needs separate processes. rcl=partial: the transport layer is verified end-to-end; the full per-type/service/action Linux capability matrix is not yet individually run. |
| platform.windows-android | supported | missing | n-a-by-design | Windows/Android | n-a | na | na | na | na | pure-Swift wire (zenoh-pico) ships on Windows x86_64 and Android arm64/x86_64; RCL there is out of scope (MZ6 conditional on upstream rmw_zenoh Windows; Android full-rcl cross-build unsolved). |
